programmes.show_description: On July 4, 2017 Jorge Rodriguez is shot while buying tomatoes for a family event in Washington. He dies seeking help. Days later, 17-year-old Pedro Cardenas is arrested after a tip-off.
Evie Adams longed to recreate the happy, loving family she'd found in her adoptive parents, but the man in her life wanted only to destroy it.
Dr Natalia Spierings struggles to see acne hidden with makeup by a patient. Dr Toby Nelson is shocked by a patient's DIY attempt on a cyst. Dr Adil Sheraz tackles a stubborn lipoma.
Witnessing a car crime bonanza captured by drones, CCTV, dashcams, mobile phones and police bodycams - from road raging drivers to ram raids gone wrong and high-tech car thieves.
"Team Wiki Woo" competes against the Eggheads.
The "Think Tank" tries to defeat the Eggheads.

Shop JML, the home for clever ideas.
Singer Laura Elen begins to plan the biggest day of her life - getting wed! With just six months to go, she needs to get planning, starting with the florist.
Singer Nikki G prepares for an emotionally-charged performance after losing her performance partner Stevie and raising 1500 euros for a cancer charity in his honour.
In Graeme Hall's first case he turns marriage counsellor to break up a furry love triangle. And at a retirement home for rescue dogs, a resident Romanian bites the hand that feeds him.
In an episode focusing on young drivers, a silver Astra leads Dan on a 100mph chase down before U-turning into oncoming traffic.
In an episode focusing on shoplifters, Julian pursues the users of a getaway car that crashed, and James arrests the same man twice.
Police armed with tasers take part in an operation to arrest a man accused of assaulting a pregnant woman, and a car hits 80mph in a 30 zone before crashing.
Crime documentary series. In this episode, interceptor Barry outsmarts a disqualified driver and Carl makes a jaw-dropping discovery when he pulls a car over in the early hours.
Barry hunts a lad wanted for assault, the police are on the tail of a suspected drug dealer and a drink driver tries everything to avoid giving a breath sample.
A foul-mouthed pensioner pushes Barry's patience to the limit, and Sarah Capes gives a bad boy biker an early birthday present.

programmes.show_name: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car...
programmes.show_description: ...Back! Specialist car recovery companies help local governments and businesses take care of illegal or dangerous vehicles by getting them off the road.
Tane's world is turned upside down. Can Jo really trust her new boyfriend? Cash fears for his friend.
Harper's guilt surges. Cash struggles with the rules. Sonny's frustration gets the better of him.
The victim of a road traffic accident is unaware that he might have a broken neck.
A global internet outage causes systems to go down so the team are back to basics. In paediatrics, Henry, has fallen off a climbing and shattered his front teeth.
A man suffering a suspected cardiac arrest collapses at the wheel, crashing his car. And a holiday ends abruptly when a man tumbles out of the hot tub in a remote log cabin.
An elderly man is in dire straits after being run over by a motorcycle. A man struggles after being stabbed multiple times, including in the neck.
Dr Emma McMullen has a painful nail condition to diagnose; Dr Natalia Spierings tries to figure out why a shoulder lump is oily; and Dr Adil Sheraz deals with a mole on a mole.
Boozed-up Britain at its worst, from a bouncer racially abused by a clubber to a roadside rumble between a late-night drinker and a taxi driver whose car has been damaged.
Emergency situations compel Dr Beecraft, Dr Glynne and Dr Swanne to refer their patients for specialist treatment.
Paramedics arrive with eight-month-old Noah, who is struggling to breathe. Kathleen, 78, who fell over, requires a CT scan and stitches for deep wounds by her eye.
Dr Adil Sheraz treats a bus driver with over 100 lipomas, Dr Toby Nelson tries to help a 23-year-old who's covered in keloids, plus a treat of a cyst for Dr Emma McMullen.
The FF9 Friends challenge the Eggheads.

Tribute singer Paige takes on her most ambitious fitness challenge to date - her first ever half marathon. Meanwhile, dog rescuer Susie searches for homes for seven abandoned puppies.
Sunshine Radio presenter Simon juggles early mornings and late nights as he swaps the studio for the stage, performing as Elvis and Frank Sinatra on the Costa Blanca.
Graeme Hall meets Toy Poodle Ralph who, after being hit by a car, has fallen out with owner Karina. And noisy wire-haired Dachshund Tulah clucks like a chicken on every car trip!
The Traffic Cops come up against some of the most dangerous and vicious criminals operating on their North Yorkshire patch.
Documentary series. A violent new crime wave known as County Lines is taking hold in Britain. Across the country, urban drug gangs are expanding their empires into small towns.
A fifth of the offences which North Yorkshire Police deal with, like theft and drug-dealing, are committed by crooks from outside the area.
Jim Woolerton goes hot on the trail of a speeding motorcyclist and Jon Moon reveals his guilty pleasure when he retrieves a stolen caravan. (S8 Ep )
Jim intercepts a car containing cannabis, a late-night reveller talks himself into trouble and Sergeant Baxter is confronted by a dishonest driver.
The interceptors stop an uninsured driver with a violent past. Jon Peacock and his dog Brad hunt for suspected burglars. Sarah Capes smells something illegal.

programmes.show_name: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car...
programmes.show_description: ...Back! Following car recovery companies that are tasked with taking illegal, unroadworthy, untaxed, uninsured vehicles off the road and ensuring their owners pay the penalty.
Harper's guilt surges. Cash struggles with the rules. Sonny's frustration gets the better of him.
Remi pays a long overdue visit. Justin and Leah rekindle the romance. Is Mali right to not trust Holden?
A young couple need confirmation that they are expecting their first baby, and a woman with a history of postnatal depression is worried about her fourth pregnancy.
Four-year-old Skyler is rushed into Paediatrics care with recurring seizures; 65-year-old Shaun has tumbled down some steps; and Eric, 85, has a badly swollen leg.
Shocking new series following medics battling to save the lives of people injured on Britain's deadly roads. In this episode, Martin suffers a catastrophic heart attack at the wheel... (Ep1)
When Duncan is admitted to Royal Stoke emergency department after falling down a full flight of stairs onto concrete, trauma team leader Richard Hall fears the worst.
